rag_rules: Preserve architecture by adding behavior only within the selected owner scope and leaving existing components, features, connections, and properties unchanged.
Keep type-level content attached only to symbols visible in that type scope; implementation-only references belong only in implementation scope when valid.
If retrieved guidance mixes type-scope and implementation-scope patterns, retain only the scope-compatible content for the chosen owner and discard invalid cross-scope references.
generic_rules: Do not change structural AADL elements when inserting behavioral content; preserve owners and existing declarations exactly.
Respect owner boundaries: content added to a component type must use only names visible there, while implementation-scoped content may rely on implementation-local structure only when allowed.
Prefer the minimal insertion that satisfies scope rules and avoids moving unrelated declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL scope validity matters more than behavioral intent during fusion; keep inserted blocks within the owner's legal scope.
When guidance refers to subcomponent paths or implementation-local names, treat them as invalid for type-owned content.
anti_patterns: Do not insert content under the wrong owner or rewrite architecture to make a behavioral reference visible.
Do not copy example-specific equations, guarantees, or identifier paths from retrieved cards into the fused model.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-specific type diagnostics and AGREE scalar type-name troubleshooting
Requirement semantics for specific signal relationships
Example contract patterns for selector logic or output clamping
